heading: Recruitment and retention of individuals who have lived, worked, or studied in predominantly Muslim countries or communities
label: 2734d Recruitment and retention of individuals who have lived, worked, or studied in predominantly Muslim countries or communities
notes: DefinitionsFor definitions of “Department” and “Secretary” as used in this section, see , set out as a note under .section 2 of Pub. L. 114–323section 2651 of this title

children.heading: Findings
children.label: a Findings
children.text: Congress finds that successful engagement, including robust public diplomacy, with predominantly Muslim countries and communities is critical for achieving United States foreign policy objectives.

children.heading: Sense of Congress
children.label: b Sense of Congress
children.text: It is the sense of Congress that the Department should recruit more employees that have a personal background in, and thorough understating of, the cultures, languages, and history of the Middle East and wider Muslim world.1So in original. Probably should be “understanding”.1

children.heading: Recruitment and retention of certain individuals
children.label: c Recruitment and retention of certain individuals
children.text: The Secretary shall make every effort to recruit and retain individuals that have lived, worked, or studied in predominantly Muslim countries or communities, including individuals who have studied at an Islamic institution of higher learning.
